violations.text: Raw animal foods (beef) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (cheese) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. PIC relocated foods. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2024
Ground beef was marked with a day or date that exceeded 7 days. PIC voluntarily disposed of food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Cooked chicken was placed hot in the refrigerator in covered plastic containers greater than four inches deep. Chicken was uncovered and moved to the walk-in to properly cool. PIC to review cooling methods with staff. New Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2024

Cake packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, allergens, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. New Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2024

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of dry ingredients. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. PIC disposed of bowls/cups. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2024

The sink used for food preparation is lacking an air gap (has air break.) A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 12/05/2024

Food contact surfaces of the veggie slicer were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Slicer was properly washed/rinsed/sanitized at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2023

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of PIC, Spaghetti was observed placed in individual covered plastic containers at 61 degrees Fahrenheit. Based on time of cooking and temperature observed, PIC moved spaghetti to the walk-in cooler and uncovered for rapid cooling at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2023
Refrigerator door gasket(s) to the 3 Door Low Boy Pizza Prep unit were observed in poor repair (torn/peeling). Equipment is to be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 12/04/2023
